div.style: display: inline-block; background-color: #d9e0e7; padding: 6px; margin: 4px; border: 1px solid #8da1b4; border-radius: 5px; line-height: 60%
div.p: version : 1; Dernière mise à jour : 2026-01-07 10:12:52+0100; Langue : en

p.b: Cette terminologie de référence (CodeSystem) définit les propriétés suivantes pour ses concepts

tr.td: The internal identifier for the concept in the HL7 Access database repository.
Indicates that the code is abstract - only intended to be used as a selector for other concepts
Designation of a concept's state. Normally is not populated unless the state is retired.

div.p: This case-sensitive code system http://terminology.hl7.org/CodeSystem/v3-substanceAdminSubstitution defines the following codes dans une hiérarchie Is-A:
Substitution occurred or is permitted with another product that may potentially have different ingredients, but having the same biological and therapeutic effects.
Substitution occurred or is permitted with another bioequivalent and therapeutically equivalent product.
Substitution occurred or is permitted with another product that is a:

ul.li: pharmaceutical alternative containing the same active ingredient but is formulated with different salt, ester
pharmaceutical equivalent that has the same active ingredient, strength, dosage form and route of administration
Pharmaceutical alternative: Erythromycin Ethylsuccinate for Erythromycin Stearate

div.p: Substitution occurred or is permitted between equivalent Brands but not Generics
Substitution occurred or is permitted between equivalent Generics but not Brands
Substitution occurred or is permitted with another product having the same therapeutic objective and safety profile.
Substitution occurred or is permitted between therapeutically equivalent Brands but not Generics
Substitution occurred or is permitted between therapeutically equivalent Generics but not Brands
This substitution was performed or is permitted based on formulary guidelines.
No substitution occurred or is permitted.

description.value: Identifies what sort of change is permitted or has occurred between the therapy that was ordered and the therapy that was/will be provided.

copyright.value: This material derives from the HL7 Terminology THO. THO is copyright ©1989+ Health Level Seven International and is made available under the CC0 designation. For more licensing information see: https://terminology.hl7.org/license.html

description.value: The internal identifier for the concept in the HL7 Access database repository.
Indicates that the code is abstract - only intended to be used as a selector for other concepts
Designation of a concept's state. Normally is not populated unless the state is retired.

definition.value: Substitution occurred or is permitted with another product that may potentially have different ingredients, but having the same biological and therapeutic effects.
Substitution occurred or is permitted with another bioequivalent and therapeutically equivalent product.

Substitution occurred or is permitted with another product that is a:
* pharmaceutical alternative containing the same active ingredient but is formulated with different salt, ester * pharmaceutical equivalent that has the same active ingredient, strength, dosage form and route of administration
*Examples:*
* *Pharmaceutical alternative:* Erythromycin Ethylsuccinate for Erythromycin Stearate * *Pharmaceutical equivalent:* Lisonpril for Zestril

Substitution occurred or is permitted between equivalent Brands but not Generics
*Examples:*
* Zestril for Prinivil * Coumadin for Jantoven

Substitution occurred or is permitted between equivalent Generics but not Brands
*Examples:*
* Lisnopril (Lupin Corp) for Lisnopril (Wockhardt Corp)

Substitution occurred or is permitted with another product having the same therapeutic objective and safety profile.
*Examples:*
* ranitidine for Tagamet

Substitution occurred or is permitted between therapeutically equivalent Brands but not Generics
>
*Examples:*
* Zantac for Tagamet

Substitution occurred or is permitted between therapeutically equivalent Generics but not Brands
>
*Examples:*
* Ranitidine for cimetidine
This substitution was performed or is permitted based on formulary guidelines.
No substitution occurred or is permitted.
